methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $182M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Standard Nuclear, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Kurt Terrani, operates within a sector represented by 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $182M to date.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to peer set constraints. The Stage-Progression approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $182M total raised proxy, reflecting typical valuation expansion from Series A to the next financing stage. This yields an implied valuation of $364M. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while 8 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, similar valuation tier) produced insufficient overlap—fewer than 3 datapoints—making a sector-wide median unreliable without introducing material distortion.

The triangulated range is $309M to $419M, with a median of $364M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the adequacy of the stage-progression multiplier for the nuclear subsector specifically, the absence of direct peer valuation anchors, and Standard Nuclear's differentiation relative to the broader competitor set. Further valuation precision would require either tighter peer comparables or substantive margin and growth assumptions tied to underlying unit economics.
